subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards
- **1 main game board** — Depicts post-earthquake Lisbon with the city grid, harbor, church influence track, royal favor tracks, and noble court.
- **4 player boards** — Personal tableaus tracking wigs (influence), rubble, treasury, and portfolio.

#### Cards
- **Treasury cards** — Multi-use cards representing nobles and their associated actions. Each card can be played as either a Treasury action (to the noble's area) or a Commerce action.
- **Royal favor cards** — Bonus cards earned through political influence.
- **Decree cards** — Government decrees that modify rules or scoring.

#### Tokens and Markers
- **Rubble cubes** — Placed on city lots. Must be cleared before building.
- **Shop tiles** — Built in city lots after clearing rubble.
- **Public building tiles** — Major constructions that provide area bonuses.
- **Influence markers** — Wigs (influence tokens) placed with the Marquis, King, or Church.
- **Ship tokens** — Used for trade and goods transport.
- **Goods cubes** — Various trade goods.
- **VP markers** — Track victory points.
- **Clergy, noble, and royal meeples** — Key figures in the game.

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
#### City Setup
1. **Place main board** — Set the board showing destroyed Lisbon.
2. **Rubble placement** — Place rubble cubes on designated city lots.
3. **Noble court** — Arrange the three nobles (Marquis of Pombal, the King, the Patriarch/Church) in their court positions.
4. **Influence tracks** — Set influence markers at starting positions.
5. **Decree cards** — Draw and place active decrees for the game.
6. **Ship track** — Place ships at the harbor.
7. **Public buildings** — Stack public building tiles in their supply.

#### Card Setup
- **Treasury deck** — Shuffle all treasury cards and deal starting hands.
- **Display** — Place a row of face-up treasury cards as an available display.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Starting Hand and Resources
- **Starting cards** — Each player receives a starting hand of treasury cards.
- **Starting wigs** — A small number of influence markers (wigs).
- **Starting rubble** — Players begin with some rubble on their player board (representing personal resources).
- **Portfolio** — Empty at start; tracks your built shops and public buildings.

#### Turn Order
- **Determined by setup** — Initial turn order is set during setup. Turn order shifts based on gameplay.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
#### Core Concept
Lisboa recreates the rebuilding of Lisbon after the devastating earthquake, tsunami, and fire of 1755. Players use multi-purpose cards to interact with three powerful historical figures: the Marquis of Pombal (city planning), the King (royal decrees and public works), and the Patriarch (church influence). Every card can be played in two fundamentally different ways, creating deep strategic decisions.

#### Game Arc
- **Card-driven** — The game revolves around playing cards from your hand.
- **City rebuilding** — Players clear rubble and build shops in Lisbon's grid.
- **Political influence** — Gaining favor with the three leaders provides ongoing benefits and VP.
- **Multiple paths** — Commerce, construction, political influence, and shipping all provide VP.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Options
On your turn, you must play exactly ONE card from your hand. You choose how to play it:

**Option A: Treasury Action (Noble Visit)**
- Play the card face-up to the matching noble's area (Marquis, King, or Patriarch based on card color).
- Gain the noble's favor action depending on which noble you visit.
- **Marquis** — City planning: clear rubble, open city lots, gain building permits.
- **King** — Royal decree: gain decrees, acquire public building plans, receive royal favors.
- **Patriarch** — Church influence: advance on the church track, gain clergy bonuses, influence cardinals.

**Option B: Commerce Action**
- Play the card face-down to your personal portfolio area.
- Use the card's commerce value to perform trade actions: acquire goods, sell goods, build shops, hire ships.

#### Card Acquisition
- **Draw cards** — After playing a card, draw 1 or more cards from the display or deck.
- **Display refresh** — The card display refreshes as cards are taken.
- **Hand management** — Balancing when to play cards for Treasury vs. Commerce is the central tension.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Rebuilding the City
- **Clear rubble** — Spend actions to remove rubble cubes from city lots. Rubble goes to your player board (used as resources later).
- **Build shops** — After clearing a lot, build a shop tile there. Shops generate income and VP.
- **Shop adjacency** — Shops adjacent to public buildings score bonus VP.
- **Street bonuses** — Completing a street (row of shops) triggers bonus scoring.

#### Public Buildings
- **Construction** — Build major public buildings in the city using plans acquired from the King.
- **Area influence** — Public buildings affect all adjacent shops, potentially benefiting multiple players.
- **VP value** — Public buildings are worth significant VP.

#### Political Influence
- **Wigs (influence)** — Place wigs with the Marquis, King, or Patriarch to gain their favor.
- **Favor tracks** — Advancing on favor tracks provides ongoing benefits and end-game scoring.
- **Clergy** — The Patriarch's track provides access to clergy bonuses and church scoring.

#### Trade and Commerce
- **Goods** — Acquire goods through commerce card plays.
- **Ships** — Use ships to transport goods for profit.
- **Market** — Sell goods at the market for VP and income.
- **Portfolio building** — Commerce cards in your portfolio contribute to end-game scoring.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Rubble Management
- **Dual purpose** — Rubble removed from the city goes to your player board. Rubble on your player board serves as a resource for certain actions.
- **City rubble** — Prevents building until cleared. Clearing is a necessary step.
- **Player rubble** — Can be spent to reduce costs of certain constructions.

#### The Three Nobles
- **Marquis of Pombal** — Controls city planning. Visiting him lets you manipulate the city grid, gain building permits, and clear rubble efficiently.
- **King Jose I** — Controls royal favors. Visiting him gives access to decrees (ongoing rule modifications) and public building plans.
- **Patriarch** — Controls church influence. Visiting him advances your religious standing, which scores VP and provides unique bonuses.

#### Decrees
- **Rule modifications** — Decree cards change or enhance game rules for the player who holds them.
- **Acquisition** — Obtained through the King's favor.
- **Variety** — Decrees can affect scoring, costs, actions, or provide new abilities.

#### Ships and Trade
- **Ship capacity** — Each ship can carry a certain amount of goods.
- **Trade routes** — Selling goods via ships provides VP bonuses.
- **Fleet management** — More ships enable more profitable trading.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
- **Deck exhaustion** — The game ends when the treasury card deck is depleted and all players have had equal turns.

#### Final Scoring
- **Shops** — VP for each shop built in the city, with adjacency bonuses for public buildings.
- **Public buildings** — VP for each public building constructed.
- **Influence tracks** — VP based on position on each noble's favor track.
- **Church track** — VP from church influence advancement.
- **Portfolio** — VP from commerce cards in your portfolio.
- **Decrees** — VP from certain decree cards.
- **Goods and ships** — Remaining goods and ships may provide VP.
- **Wigs** — Remaining influence tokens may score.
- **Street bonuses** — VP for completed streets.

#### Winner
- **Highest VP** — Player with the most victory points wins.
- **Tiebreaker** — Player with more remaining cards in hand.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Every card is two options** — Always consider both Treasury and Commerce before playing. The right choice depends on timing.
- **Build shops adjacent to public buildings** — The adjacency bonus is significant and compounds.
- **Don't neglect influence** — The favor tracks provide ongoing benefits. Falling behind on influence is hard to recover from.
- **Rubble is a resource** — Don't rush to clear all rubble. Rubble on your board has uses.
- **Watch what opponents need** — Blocking city lots or taking key cards from the display can be very effective.
- **Diversify scoring** — Lisboa rewards players who score across multiple categories rather than focusing on just one.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
#### Strategy 1: City Builder
- **Goal** — Focus on clearing rubble and building shops for city-based VP.
- **Key actions** — Marquis visits for permits, Commerce for shop construction.
- **Benefit** — Shops provide steady VP and income.

#### Strategy 2: Political Influence
- **Goal** — Advance on all three influence tracks for maximum favor bonuses and end-game scoring.
- **Key actions** — Treasury plays to nobles, wig placement.
- **Benefit** — High influence provides compound benefits each turn.

#### Strategy 3: Trade Empire
- **Goal** — Build a shipping fleet and trade goods for VP.
- **Key actions** — Commerce plays for ships and goods, market sales.
- **Benefit** — Profitable trading generates both money and VP.

#### General Principles
- **Card flow** — Keep your hand full. Running out of good card options limits your turns.
- **Timing** — Early influence pays off longer; late-game building can score big in final scoring.
- **Flexibility** — Adapt your strategy to the cards you draw and the decrees available.
